title: Grover's Search Algorithm

theory.introduction: Grover's Algorithm provides a quadratic speedup for searching an unsorted database. While classical search requires O(N) operations, Grover's algorithm finds the target element in approximately O(√N) steps using amplitude amplification.
key_points: Grover's algorithm amplifies probability of the correct answer
Uses an oracle to mark the correct state
Diffusion operator increases probability amplitude of marked state
Demonstrates clear quantum speedup over classical search

problem_definition.description: Given an unsorted database of N elements, find the marked element using minimal queries.

core_idea.description: Start in equal superposition of all possible states. Iteratively increase probability amplitude of correct state using interference.

superposition.description: Apply Hadamard gates to create equal probability distribution across all states.

oracle.description: Marks the correct solution by flipping its phase.

diffusion_operator.description: Reflects amplitudes about their average, increasing probability of marked state.

steps.description: Apply Hadamard gates to create equal superposition
Apply Oracle to mark correct state
Apply Diffusion operator to amplify amplitude

mathematical_representation.oracle_operation: Oracle flips phase of target state

circuit_examples.name: 2-Qubit Grover Example
circuit_examples.description: Search for one marked state among four possible states
circuit_examples.expected_result: Probability of target state approaches 1 after one iteration

circuit_examples.name: Iteration Visualization
circuit_examples.description: Probability of correct state increases after each iteration

amplitude_amplification.description: Grover's algorithm increases amplitude of correct state using constructive interference.

geometric_interpretation.description: Algorithm can be viewed as rotation toward target state in vector space.

limitations: Requires knowledge of approximate solution count
summary: Grover's algorithm uses amplitude amplification to efficiently search unsorted data. It demonstrates a quadratic speedup compared to classical algorithms, highlighting the power of quantum interference.

try_this: Notice that the target state |11⟩ has the highest probability after one Grover iteration
Compare with equal superposition (just H on both) — all states have equal 25% probability
Grover's algorithm boosts one state from 25% to near 100%

quiz.question: What is the classical complexity of searching an unsorted database of N elements?
quiz.explanation: Classical search requires checking each element one by one — O(N) in the worst case.

quiz.question: What is Grover's quantum complexity for searching N elements?
quiz.explanation: Grover's algorithm requires approximately √N iterations, giving a quadratic speedup.

quiz.question: What does the Oracle do in Grover's algorithm?
options: Flips the phase of the target state
quiz.answer: Flips the phase of the target state
quiz.explanation: The Oracle marks the correct answer by negating its amplitude: Uω|x⟩ = -|x⟩ for the solution.

quiz.question: What does the Diffusion operator do?
options: Amplifies the probability of the marked state
quiz.answer: Amplifies the probability of the marked state
quiz.explanation: Amplitude amplification reflects all amplitudes about their average, boosting the marked state.
